name: Stuffed Bean Curd with Peas
recipeIngredient: 4 Square pieces of fresh hard beancurd
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine ground pork, soy sauce, sherry, garlic, green onion, ginger juice, and sesame oil in a bowl. Cover and set aside.
2. Heat frying oil in a wok until very hot (test with a bamboo chopstick; vigorous bubbles should form around it).
3. Carefully add beancurd squares to the hot oil and fry until browned. Remove and drain on paper towels.
4. Cut each square in half diagonally.
5. Using a paring knife, carefully create a hollow pocket in the center of each beancurd piece.
6. Fill the pockets with about 1 tablespoon of the meat mixture.
7. Dust the exposed surface of each stuffed beancurd piece with cornstarch.
8. Return to the hot oil and fry until the coating is browned and crispy. Drain on paper towels.
9. In a clay pot, combine chicken broth, stuffed deep-fried beancurd, peas, and mushrooms. Bring to a boil, cover, reduce heat, and simmer for 10 minutes.
10. Uncover, return to a boil briefly, and thicken the soup slightly with cornstarch paste. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
11. Serve hot.
